meaning_english: A former student of a particular school, college, or university.
One who does something for pleasure and not as a profession.
A diplomatic representative of the highest order sent by one country to another.
A person appointed by two parties to settle a dispute.
A group of people gathered to hear a speech or performance.
An instrument used for measuring atmospheric pressure.
An instrument for measuring time accurately, especially at sea.
An instrument used for measuring the humidity of the air.
An instrument for detecting and recording earthquakes.
A large bedroom with a number of beds for a number of people.
A building where threshed grain is stored.
An official who executes people by hanging them.
A large building with an extensive floor area, typically for housing aircraft.
A person skilled in foreign languages.
One who dies for a noble cause.
A person who dislikes humankind and avoids human society.
A long speech by one actor in a play or movie.
A person who has an excessive interest in or admiration of themselves.
A person new to and inexperienced in a job or situation.
A notice of a death, especially in a newspaper.
An animal or person that eats food of both plant and animal origin.
A person who believes that war and violence are unjustifiable.
A person walking rather than travelling in a vehicle.
Occurring, awarded, or appearing after the death of the originator.
The practice of taking someone else's work or ideas and passing them off as one's own.
An establishment for the medical treatment of people who are convalescing or have a chronic illness.
A building set apart and adapted for keeping horses.
The branch of science concerned with the nature, effects, and detection of poisons.
A person who lends money at unreasonably high rates of interest.
A person who freely offers to take part in an enterprise or undertake a task.
